hook: ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.

I didn't ask for a framework. I didn't ask for headers. I definitely didn't ask for a section called 'Step 7: Consider Your Tone.'

I asked for help with one email.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI isn't bad at writing. It's bad at knowing *you're* the kind of person who wants a draft, not a debrief.

Some people want the reasoning. They want to understand the 'why' before they touch anything.

Some people want you to just DO it. Give me the words. I'll decide if they're right.

AI treats everyone like the first type. Because that's the 'safe' response. It's thorough. It's defensible. It's also completely exhausting if you're not wired that way.

BootFile fixes this by building a profile of how you actually think — including whether you want the map or just the destination.

Stop re-explaining yourself every single session.

Link in bio.
